D5 Design L2 Understand Atom

Newton's Musical Wheel

Newton bent the spectrum into a circle and sized each colour by a note of the scale — so the sectors are deliberately unequal

Newton's 1704 colour circle was the first. He believed colour and music shared a harmony, so he sized the seven sectors by the intervals of the Dorian scale (D-E-F-G-A-B-C). The two semitone steps — E→F and B→C — make the orange and indigo sectors visibly narrower.

Switch between an equal seven-way split and Newton's musical division. In musical mode each sector carries its note; the narrow orange and indigo arcs are the semitones. The unevenness is the whole point — colour was tuned, not measured.
